    We talked about what the course would include - Focus on small teaching, commitment to techniques, how do we relate this to the age of AI
    We talked about the differences between Coursera and Canvas Expand - the importance of having a broader reach was a big consideration 
    We talked through when we would like to have the course - Fall semester 2026 would be ideal

We have since made the decision that we're going to use coursera. We meet next week to start making progress towards the design of the course.

title: Sprint - Unlocking Inaccessible Korean Heritage - Jul 13
text:
Summary

Great meeting on Thursday. It looks like the experience is taking shape and Hana and Chaoli are happy with the direction. 

    Team is finalizing the murals. Chaoli and his graduate students are looking to super-enhance some of the murals because the originals are very faded
    Colin has started "modernizing" the murals that are ready. For example, there is a ceiling mural that will be used in each chamber. He has started working on this. Eventually, he will "modernize" the rest of the murals including the superenhanced ones
    Hana will work on scripts for all of the audio that is needed. We decided that the Korean AI voice is expressive enough for some of the explanatory and introduction scripts
    The next meeting is in two weeks because of conference conflicts in scheduling

----------------------------------------

author: Yi Lu
title: Sprint - Unlocking Inaccessible Korean Heritage - Jun 29
text:

To decide on the number of quiz questions and gameplay, Noah and Adam will come up with a prototype for Hana's kids to test during the next meeting, e.g., difficulty level and how long it might take to finish. We will further narrow down the content after we test the prototype. 

----------------------------------------

author: Michael Lerma
title: Sprint - Unlocking Inaccessible Korean Heritage - Jun 22
text:
Summary

Meeting went well and the team has a solid plan on proceeding. 

    We talked through the user flow the team came up with 
        Three-chambered approach with each being one of the themes (art, daily life, mythical creatures)
        Each chamber will have certain things we need to find from the murals
        Once everything is found there will be some kind of activity
    We talked through the logistics of having students go through the experience. It could be during one class period. We could have 5-10 minutes of logistics and then 3 rotations of 20 minutes so that all of the students can go through the VR component
Next meeting on Thursday

----------------------------------------

Next steps

    Ted drafted and sent an email to more than 8,000 Coursera students he have taken other ND courses. There was a nice bump in enrollments not long after that.

    We updated Gabriel on the course stats, which are actually a little better than the average religious studies course. That and the various plans we have for promotion and for improving engagement/retention seemed to reassure Gabriel.

She said we should also be promoting that ND was recognized as having the #1 Theology department on the homepage of this course and linking to the course in all relevant places.

title: The Qur’an and the Bible (Gabriel Reynolds) - Mar 27
text:
Summary
    Gabriel was away/unavailable to meet this week.

    We were hoping he'd start to enter data in the sandbox Yi created for the Dialogue/AI coaching tool, but he has not gotten to that yet. (Late addition: Gabriel—with Yi having teed things up for him—followed through on the Dialogue draft work, with Yi immediately using Gabriel's material to prompt ChatGPT to draft rubrics for assessing advanced-, intermediate-, and beginner-level performance in the course.)

    Total enrollment is up to 256, which is around 12% higher than this time last week with six people having completed the course. 

    On overall lessons, there is 100% thumbs up feedback, and on specific videos, readings, etc., there is a 98% thumbs up rate.

    Yi and Lawrence responded to all learner comments/requests, and there have been no "religious warrior" type comments so far.

    Coursera was supposed to have included our course in a LinkedIn post on Thursday, but as of this writing (2:00 PM Thursday), nothing is up. (Late update: The post went up Friday midday with Ted jumping on the relinking.)

    Ted helped prompt a really nice ThinkND post this past week.


Next Steps
    We'll continue to push forward on the Dialogue tool.

    Both Gabriel and Ted will continue with promotion.

    We'll be one month from launch at the end of this week at which point Coursera said it could run some benchmarking against similar courses.

text:
Summary
Yi prepared Gabriel to develop data for use with the Dialogue student-coaching tool. We'll test out the process on one module and build from what we learn there.

Enrollment is up to 225 (as of 10:30 AM Friday). Gabriel had seemed disappointed with the numbers, but Alex from Coursera told us that the numbers are solid and that they rarely see a surge when a course is launched and that we should first benchmark against similar courses at the one-month mark (April 5).

Promotion continues—with a blurb going out in the Alumni Newsletter last week.

Next Steps
We'll continue development with the Dialogue.

Promotion this week includes Coursera highlighting the course on its 3/27 LinkedIn post about new content. Gabriel has recorded two podcasts, and we're waiting to hear when those go live.

title: Status update - Mar 14
text:
Summary
Gabriel had to cancel last minute, so we'll pick up this week focusing on developing the Dialogue (student coaching) tool in Coursera.

Gabriel seemed a little down about the enrollment numbers (205 as of the morning of 3/17/25) but needs to realize that this paid course is going to get many fewer students than his EdX course, which was free. There is also a good deal of promotion to come.

In the meantime, it has received 101 thumbs up on content likes and only 2 thumbs down.

Two students commented about the background music in one section being too loud so that it interferes with their being able to hear the spoken content. Is there anything we can do about that?

Next Steps
Again, we'll be meeting with Gabriel to see about integrating the Dialogue tool in the course, and both Ted and Gabriel have promotion opportunities on the horizon.

title: Status update - Mar 6
text:
Summary
The course and promotion launched on March 5.

As of this writing (Thursday at 10:00 AM) there are 77 people enrolled with a 97% thumbs up rate on feedback so far.

Yi presented Gabriel with a number of possible Use Cases for deploying the Coursera Dialogue tool (an AI-based, individualized personal coach for students).

We have an ask in Coursera about setting up a sandbox for the Dialogue tool so that we can test and develop it.

We noted that there are already "Religious Warriors" commenting in various channels and forums about the course. This will need continued monitoring, and our preparing of the Dialogue tool will need to consider "Religious Warriors" weaponizing their Dialogue process.

Next Steps
Yi and Gabriel will partner on preparing and deploying the Dialogue tool.

Next steps

text:

Brian and Kuangchen will, as time and inclination permit, review, revise, and or add to Leo's objectives. And Leo's work will certainly be used long term as we learn from this pilot implementation.

Leo's work (which will likely not conclude anytime soon) will not effect the actual course map, which—basically—reflects the traditional ASCEND learning content.

We will, however, use the Motivation idea videos to add a very direct engineering approach to problem solving following an analysis process.

Next steps

On 3/7 we'll have a media pre-production meeting with Matthew and Victoria Goodrich, who will be the faculty member presenting the first of the Motivation videos that will serve as a model for the others.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Status update - Feb 6
text:

We reviewed and revised the course goals.

Next steps

The team will complete the process of ensuring the learning content aligns with the course goals. Once that is done, Kuangchen will coordinate the evaluation process with Alex/ND Learning and lead us through the finalization of the course map.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Status update - Jan 31
text:
Summary
Brian has completed the course map.

The course content will be an engineering-focused tweak to the traditional ASCEND program. The key adjustments not previously detailed will include:
    A short video before each of the learning content sessions (8) in which a campus stakeholder presents an engineering-focused challenge or problem but then indicates that solving the problem starts with math that involves Topic X—which will be the topic of that session's content.
    The small group application problems will be split into two parts—analysis and design. Some of these problems will need to be significantly revised/redone from the original ASCEND problems.




Next steps
We will review and finalize the course map in next week's session, including asking/challenging ourselves to state the program's objectives and desired outcomes and assuring ourselves that the course map/plans align with those.

Media kickoff session is scheduled for 2/14, but KC will be joining our 2/6 meeting to make sure we are all on the same page with what is possible.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Status update - Jan 24
text:
Summary
This project has been moved from "At risk" status because it has received basic approval and is trending in the right direction. There are still issues to be resolved, including precisely how students will be identified for invitation into the program.

In terms of the content, Brian and Leo agreed that the best way forward for this year is to go with the basic ASCEND program, tweaking the material to give it an engineering focus.

That tweaking would be informed by the CRT results (and analysis of those results, including that provided by IRIS) and a pre-existing math diagnostic the students would take (ALEKS) prior to the start of the program.

Participating students would be encouraged to take ALEKS again after the program. Data from these pre- and post-tests would help inform future and further development of the program.

In addition to the tweaked current ASCEND learning content, we anticipate creating three of four videos of ND professors describing how the math being learned in the ASCEND Engineering program would be put to use in their classrooms/courses.

Sonia is using a program proposal that we created to gather further support and clarity on this project.

Next steps
    Finalize the project proposal and lock down the non-learning design or media production details and logistics of the program going forward.

    Kuangchen will work with Brian and Leo to detail the course map.

    Brian and Leo are looking into how to best utilize the ALEKS diagnostic tool.

    Media Production has the anticipated asset needs for this project on its radar.

    We're meeting next on 1/28.

text:
Summary
We met with the broader group and we got very good feedback from everyone. Adam remarked that it's like night and day between the version from years ago compared to Jacob's version. There were some suggestions to try to squeeze in before use in the classroom. We are planning on Mar 20 as the demo in the classroom and are coordinating with Adam to make sure we have enough headsets for that day. These are the suggestions:

    Controls more intuitive?
    Trigger phone a second earlier
    Change ringtone - to more generic sound
    Haptic feedback if you grab a wheel 
    Move the camera for the mirror view
    Sun as a light source to make it harder to see
    Adjustable for the height
    Test on a Quest 2

I confirmed with Jacob that he should be able to tackle these items. Susan said she is good with where we are at and doesn't require another demo before use in the classroom.

    Finalize Dashboard 
    Other things in the background
        Person walking a dog
        Animal walking around
        Potentially other cars
    Bigger car crash impact / collision feel

title: Sprint 2025 - Bringing Korean to Life - Yeonhee Yoon - Jul 18
text:
Summary
Learning content development continues
Yeonhee is requested to continue work on content updates, including more detailed guidance on bowing etiquette for male vs. female students, expanded sample dialogue for Yunsun’s Convai character, and a refined Chuseok explanation that connects the activity to ancestral rites. She is also asked to include photos for the Yutnori and work on a combined vocabulary and translation sheet, as well as a translated introductory paragraph for the hanbok section.
 
Instructional enhancements
Yi is integrating descriptive pop-up images for selected Korean vocabulary and revising the Chuseok introduction text to reflect its cultural significance better. She is also developing multiple-choice knowledge checks that will be embedded in Canvas as part of the pre- and post-class flow.
 
Visuals & technical updates
Min is continuing to debug the VR experience and refine key interactions, including the bowing sequence. She is also simplifying Yunsun’s dialogue following activity completion to make it more accessible. These visual and interaction updates are expected to be completed before her departure on August 11.
 
Timeline & Scope planning
With 1–2 weeks of QA scheduled for early August, we now have a limited window for finalizing content and gathering feedback. A focused weekly plan will be implemented to define what gets completed each week. Any additional feature or content requests outside of this scope will be placed in a parking lot for future review.

Additional Notes
    Yeonhee will not attend the August 1 meeting and will submit content updates in advance.
    Team to align on week-by-week development and QA timeline during the next check-in.


Next steps
    Yeonhee to submit final content and dialogue updates.
    Yi to continue Canvas build and quiz development.
    Min to finalize visual/interaction changes.
    Michael to confirm and share a weekly scope and delivery plan.

text:
Summary
Content development & cultural framing
Yeonhee will refine the content structure and develop a cultural introduction covering hanbok, table-setting, and bowing traditions. She will also prepare a vocabulary list with honorifics for the Hanbok section and provide matching quizzes for the pre-class activity. A video demonstrating the bowing gesture will be shared, and Yeonhee will also draft the Lesson Flow content and tag Yi ahead of the next meeting for review. Additionally, she will review Yunsun’s Convai dialogue and identify potential keywords or dialogue topics.

Instructional design & content mapping
Yi will review and update the Learning Map, add instructional videos on using VR to the pre-class section, and revise the after-class activities: removing Activity #1 and keeping Activity #2. She will also highlight areas in the content that require Yeonhee’s attention or input.

Visual & technical enhancements
Min will update visual assets, including rearranging food items for cultural accuracy (dates vs. pears), modifying mask visuals to indicate gender, and adjusting hanbok designs for realism. Min will also collaborate with Michael T. to enhance Yunsun’s appearance and adjust her Korean proficiency level to intermediate.

This is the schedule for the rest of the sprint:
    July 7 - July 18 - Welcome by Yunsun (Scene 0) & Hanbok scene (Exercise 1)
    July 21 - Aug 1 - Bowing (Exercise 3)
    Aug 2 - Aug 8 - Conversational Speaking with Yunsun (Scene 2)

    Min to implement visual adjustments and character refinements

title: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood - Oct 5
text:
Summary
I've moved this to at risk mainly because we weren't able to meet with Liz last week. Michael T is working on making sure at least two of the scenes will be working when we meet with Liz (scheduled for Thursday this week). He has had some personal events happening thus why we pushed back the date. The reduction to two scenes is to make sure we have something solid to show versus having all five scenes available.

There is additionally a Reallusion issue that Michael discovered on Friday when testing the headsets. He is targeting to have this fixed by Thursday but there is a plan B on presenting the content - it might just not be visually accurate.

We meet internally on Monday to go over the overall plan based on how everything looks

Next steps

project_name: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood
author: Michael Lerma
title: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood - Sep 7
text:
Summary
We continue to divide and conquer on the environments and avatars for the project. 

Colin is on track with the environments. We met briefly at the beginning of last week and he had 2 out of 5 done already (they look really good!)

Michael T is working on the avatars for project.

We've identified that there is a potential issue when there are 4 or more avatars used for Convai. I've flagged that in advance to Liz in order to prioritize the avatars in case we have to cut one or more avatars from the experience to ensure a stable experience. 

Our next meeting is Sept 15 for updates with Liz. End of September is when we will have Liz test them in headset for the first time. 

Next steps

project_name: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood
author: Michael Lerma
title: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood - Aug 24
text:
Summary
Marking this back as on track. We had a discussion with Liz on Friday and came up with a tentative schedule for the next couple of months:

    Mon, Aug 25 - Meet with KC and Colin to align with Colin's schedule for graphics
    Each week - both Colin and Michael T will focus on one scene (five scenes, takes us to end of Sept)
    By end of Sep - All characters will be implemented
    End of Sep (around Sep 26) - Rough test w/headsets
    Middle of Oct - Final test w/headsets
    Fri, Nov 14 - Grace Hall - 1:00PM - 3:45PM - Classroom usage for the tool

We will meet in the middle of September as an update on the characters and the end of September and middle of Oct for final testing.

Next steps

author: Michael Takami
title: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood - Aug. 4
text:
Summary
Going Smooth;y!
    More internal agent testing
        Will be meeting with Liz today for our final round of agent feedback
    Transferred core systems from BOTFL to new project
    Expanded on technical requirements documentation for this project
        i.e. What systems will have to be made, their associated programming patterns, and how they should interact with other objects
    Further diagnosed BOTFL convAI issues and am trying multiple work arounds related to environment reduction/replacement and developer tools to help further debug


Next steps
    Once this set of feedback is done we will begin in headset agent testing
    Test solutions to mitigating the BOTFL timeout issues
    Begin expanding on light scenes in Global Health, set up version control, and begin development

project_name: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood
author: Michael Takami
title: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood - July 28
text:
Summary
Going smoohtly!
    Met with Liz last week for our first round of Agent feedback
        Iterated on current agent settings and added/limited knowledge based on Liz's feedback
        Liz was not able to test all agents due to usage limit's w/ ConvAI but she now has access to the ODL account which she will be using to get more feedback for refinement this week
    User-flow and art treatment to be reviewed later this week
    Liz's project initialized in Unity
    Continued work optimizing BOTFL and addressing the visual feedback Kelly left at  our last meeting


Next steps
    This will be our last week of iteration of Agent's
        Refining knowledge bank as much as possible to fit the content needs Liz has specified within conversations
        Tailoring base prompt to better fit the response style Liz would prefer the experience to contain
    Previous core gameplay systems from BOTFL will be transferred and will begin the integration process into Liz's experience
    Finishing up work on BOTFL both functionally and visually
         ensuring that there are systems in place to diagnose when/if an error on ConvAI's end occurs on deployed devices

project_name: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood
author: Michael Takami
title: Sprint 2025 - VR in Global Health Training - Elizabeth Wood - Jul 21
text:
Summary
Going well!
    Began taking additional data sources and turning them into deep research prompts to further expand character's detail knowledge around khat and its cultivation
    Cleaned output data for increased interpretation on the agent's end along with unit testing
    Began ideating, planning, and sourcing assets related to character design/potential environments 


Next steps
    We will have 1-2 more weeks of iteration and improvement on the agents before delving into Unity development, so addressing feedback is priority 1
    On the art side, our art treatment and detailed user flow will be drafted by the end of this week. Hoping to have both agreed upon on all ends by the end of next week
    I'll be setting Liz's project up with framework code taken from BOTFL in the next week to make development more streamlined
    Talking to ConvAI about fixing the disconnection issues within BOTFL to hopefully avoid encountering those throughout the development of Liz's

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Sep 18
text:
Summary
    David Reed completed all of his Phase 1 course development work, and that was passed on to SpectrumX.
    Tom Hazlett gathered and dumped a lot of content but still has more to do.


Next steps

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Sep 14
text:
Summary
    David completed his draft course map.
    David and Lawrence met and worked on the Course Alignment document.
    Kuangchen provided feedback on some of David and Tom's materials.
    Lawrence and Tom worked synchronously on his course outline and asset list.
    Lawrence used the course map to produce David's asset list.


Next steps
    David has all of the final drafts and will review them this week with the goal of turning everything into Spectrum X by Friday.
    Tom and Lawrence will continue with synchronous working sessions over the next two weeks and then Lawrence will migrate everything into the appropriate templates.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Sep 7
text:
Summary
    David is about 90% of the way through his Course Map. He should have it done by our Tuesday meeting.
    We'll have more than a week to make any tweaks to the Course Map and develop the Course Alignment document.
    Tom completed Module 2 in his outline, but the goal was for him to be through with the outline altogether. He also needs to identify and detail more and a greater variety of learning content sources.
    Lawrence provided the Spectrum X Education and Workforce Development Group with an update on the course development process.
        The goal is to turn over the content produced by David and Tom, which would give the SX team three weeks to review it and suggest revisions and then three weeks for David and Tom


Next steps
    As indicated, David will complete his course map and use that to create the Asset List spreadsheet. We'll then use ChatGPT to draft material for his Course Alignment document for him to review and revise.
    David will have to have his outline completed and a somewhat robust asset list developed this week if we hope to be able to share his work by the 9/19 timeline.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Aug 29
text:
Summary
    David Reed made excellent progress on his course map and will focus on adding the additional resources over the next two weeks.
    Tom Hazlett redrafted his learning objectives and module overviews (awaiting review by Lawrence and Kuangchen) and will follow the format used for Module 1 to draft his course map for Modules 2 and 3. 
    Tom also needs to produce an asset list (linked to specific modules and lessons) of videos, slide shows, and readings.


Next steps
    With insight and advice from Kuangchen, Lawrence will help Tom revise his learning objectives.
    Lawrence will support Tom and David in finalizing their course maps and other documents for the week of 9/15.
    We will try to get Tom, David, and Nick on a call to review each other's outlines to ensure that the courses are aligned well with each other.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Aug 23
text:
Summary
    David Reed finished his draft of a solid Module 1 course map. We went over refinements such as a variety of assessments/activities and not just at the end of the module.
    Tom Hazlett produced a solid module 1 outline and course description, but his objectives were off target.
    Lawrence clarified the expectations of the course map—which is to be a "glorified syllabus" and does not require the content to be developed.


Next steps
    David will complete his Module 2 and 3 course map development, and then we'll go back and design a pattern for assessments and activities that he can follow throughout the course map.
    David is looking to complete the outline and draft new objectives before we pivot to the course map.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Aug 17
text:
Summary
    David Reed (CU Boulder) completed his course description, objectives, and outline, and those were shared with the SX team.
    We pivoted to work on the course map. David's course map will be fully detailed, but the content will not be fully developed by the 9/15 deadline. The goal will be to have at least a few lessons fully developed by then.

    Tom Hazlett seems much more confident than Lawrence that he will have his course description, objectives, and outline completed by Wednesday. Once that's in, Tom will make the same pivot to the course map.

    Lawrence updated Anja from SX on the status of the course development plans but has not heard back from her on any concerns.


Next steps
    Assuming that Anja/SX are comfortable with the plan for a fully detailed course map with a few lesson examples of the full content development for 9/15, we'll plow ahead with that goal.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Aug 7
text:
Summary
    Both of our faculty partners have draft course descriptions, learning objectives, and module topics drafted and should have their full outlines drafted this week and finalized next week.
    Execution of the agreements has been bottlenecked on the ND end.
    The overall director of Spectrum X is in the midst of being fired, which might actually speed things up in terms of our production.


Next steps
    Once the course outlines are completed, we'll get Nick and the other two professors together to review the outlines and address any issues or concerns regarding overlap.
    We'll transition as soon as the outlines are done to the Course Map development.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Aug 1
text:
Summary
    Kuangchen gave a thumbs up to David Reed's course overview, goals, and outline topics.
    Agreements are almost finalized with both partners.
    Tom Hazlett was prepped to complete his course overview, goals, and outline topics.


Next steps
    David will be away at a conference this week but will have his draft outline completed for review early next week.
    Tom will get feedback on his draft outline work this week and then also look to have his outline finalized by next week.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Jul 27
text:
Summary
    My update from last week seems to have disappeared?
    We onboarded Tom Hazlett from Clemson who will do a course on Spectrum economics and economic policy.
    We prepared Tom to draft a course description, goals, and module list.
    David Reed (CU—course on Spectrum Management) drafted a course description, goals, and module list and revised them with Lawrence.
    We drafted agreements between SX and David/Tom.


Next steps
    Review and revisions of David and Tom's materials (with assistance as appropriate from Kuangchen). Goal is to have the outlines completed by 8/8 and begin the course maps after that with the goal of finishing them by 9/23.
    Get the agreements fully executed.

author: Lawrence Greenspun

Summary

Next steps

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Jul 10
text:
Summary
    After two meetings to discuss, the consultation, Spectrum X leadership gave us the green light to work with Nick to recruit the faculty partners rather than working through Spectrum X leadership. This should speed up the process.

    Targeted outreach to two specific potential partners happened at the end of last week.


Next steps
    If we hear back positively from our recruitment efforts, we will look to formalize the consultation and onboard the partners this week.
    If we don't have the partners in place this week, we probably have another week of wiggle room on this but would likely have to pull the plug if those partners are not in place soon!

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Jul 4
text:
Summary
    While this potential consultation gig remains at risk, there are some positive developments:
        Lawrence will be joining a call this afternoon with the Spectrum X Education and Workforce Development Working Group (EWG) to present an overview of our offer for project management support and to help spark interest in two external faculty members to work with us to produce course development materials.


Next steps
    The original deadline to begin this project was last Friday, but Nick said that he has a week or two of wiggle room on the backend of the timeline we laid out, so we're giving him a little wiggle room on the front end to close out recruitment of the two faculty members.
    Hoping to have the faculty partners locked in place by this time next week so we can move this to "On Target" and get working on the consultation.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Consultation - Jun 29
text:
Summary
    Nick received approval from the Spectrum X Education and Workforce Development Working Group to engage ODL for project management consultation services.
    The LD Team is working on user-friendly version of the Course Outline and Course Map template to use with two external faculty partners.
    Lawrence has drafted an MOU to cover the proposed consultation.


Next steps
    Nick needs to identify and introduce us two those two external faculty partners by Thursday for this consultation to move forward. Same goes for those faculty partners hitting various benchmarks throughout the process for things to continue.

author: Lawrence Greenspun
title: Spectrum X Course Consultation - Jun 21
text:
Summary
    Nick/Spectrum X is requesting ODL's assistance with managing the process of two external institutions/SME producing course outlines and content-filled course maps for two additional Radio Systems courses by 9/15.
    The project is "At Risk" in the sense that it will not go forward unless Nick successfully recruits two colleagues that OLD approves by 7/3.


Next steps
    Nick will succeed or not succeed in recruiting partners, and the project will or will not proceed.

author: Michael Lerma
title: Diction App - Aug 24
text:
Summary
We've done an initial discovery meeting and a consultation with Stephen Lancaster from the music department. He's interested in creating an AI app that allows students to translate and hear how lyrics are sung to enhance their learning experience.

We ended up doing a two-pronged approach - 
    One is engaging with Brandon Rich in exploring the possibilities of ChatGPT and Gemini to create this app
    The other is exploring Praxis Pria and seeing if that will be a viable solution.

I think Stephen is going to use both. Brandon created an AI assistant that checks the marks for immediate usage. We will also consult with Stephen on creating a great Praxis Pria experience on his canvas course. 

Next steps
